50		// to wait for a given resource version to be propagated to cache,
51		// before terminating request and returning Timeout error with retry
52		// after suggestion.
53		blockTimeout = 3 * time.Second

55		// resourceVersionTooHighRetrySeconds is the seconds before a operation should be retried by the client
56		// after receiving a 'too high resource version' error.

60		// TODO: Figure out, to what value we can decreased it.

67	// watchCacheEvent is a single "watch event" that is send to users of
68	// watchCache. Additionally to a typical "watch.Event" it contains
69	// the previous value of the object to enable proper filtering in the
70	// upper layers.
71	type watchCacheEvent struct {
72		Type            watch.EventType
73		Object          runtime.Object
74		ObjLabels       labels.Set
75		ObjFields       fields.Set
76		PrevObject      runtime.Object
77		PrevObjLabels   labels.Set
78		PrevObjFields   fields.Set
79		Key             string
80		ResourceVersion uint64
81		RecordTime      time.Time
82	}
83	
84	// watchCache implements a Store interface.
85	// However, it depends on the elements implementing runtime.Object interface.
86	//
87	// watchCache is a "sliding window" (with a limited capacity) of objects
88	// observed from a watch.
89	type watchCache struct {
90		sync.RWMutex
91	
92		// Condition on which lists are waiting for the fresh enough
93		// resource version.
94		cond *sync.Cond
95	
96		// Maximum size of history window.
97		capacity int
98	
99		// upper bound of capacity since event cache has a dynamic size.
100		upperBoundCapacity int
101	
102		// lower bound of capacity since event cache has a dynamic size.
103		lowerBoundCapacity int
104	
105		// keyFunc is used to get a key in the underlying storage for a given object.
106		keyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error)
107	
108		// getAttrsFunc is used to get labels and fields of an object.
109		getAttrsFunc func(runtime.Object) (labels.Set, fields.Set, error)
110	
111		// cache is used a cyclic buffer - the "current" contents of it are
112		// stored in [start_index%capacity, end_index%capacity) - so the
113		// "current" contents have exactly end_index-start_index items.
114		cache      []*watchCacheEvent
115		startIndex int
116		endIndex   int
117		// removedEventSinceRelist holds the information whether any of the events
118		// were already removed from the `cache` cyclic buffer since the last relist
119		removedEventSinceRelist bool
120	
121		// store will effectively support LIST operation from the "end of cache
122		// history" i.e. from the moment just after the newest cached watched event.
123		// It is necessary to effectively allow clients to start watching at now.
124		// NOTE: We assume that <store> is thread-safe.
125		store store.Indexer
126	
127		// ResourceVersion up to which the watchCache is propagated.
128		resourceVersion uint64
129	
130		// ResourceVersion of the last list result (populated via Replace() method).
131		listResourceVersion uint64
132	
133		// This handler is run at the end of every successful Replace() method.
134		onReplace func()
135	
136		// This handler is run at the end of every Add/Update/Delete method
137		// and additionally gets the previous value of the object.
138		eventHandler func(*watchCacheEvent)
139	
140		// for testing timeouts.
141		clock clock.Clock
142	
143		// eventFreshDuration defines the minimum watch history watchcache will store.
144		eventFreshDuration time.Duration
145	
146		// An underlying storage.Versioner.
147		versioner storage.Versioner
148	
149		// cacher's group resource
150		groupResource schema.GroupResource
151	
152		// For testing cache interval invalidation.
153		indexValidator indexValidator
154	
155		// Requests progress notification if there are requests waiting for watch
156		// to be fresh
157		waitingUntilFresh *progress.ConditionalProgressRequester
158	
159		// Stores previous snapshots of orderedLister to allow serving requests from previous revisions.
160		snapshots           store.Snapshotter
161		snapshottingEnabled atomic.Bool
162	
163		getCurrentRV func(context.Context) (uint64, error)

165	
166	func newWatchCache(
167		keyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error),
168		eventHandler func(*watchCacheEvent),
169		getAttrsFunc func(runtime.Object) (labels.Set, fields.Set, error),
170		versioner storage.Versioner,
171		indexers *cache.Indexers,
172		clock clock.WithTicker,
173		eventFreshDuration time.Duration,
174		groupResource schema.GroupResource,
175		progressRequester *progress.ConditionalProgressRequester,
176		getCurrentRV func(context.Context) (uint64, error),
177	) *watchCache {
178		wc := &watchCache{
179			capacity:            defaultLowerBoundCapacity,
180			keyFunc:             keyFunc,
181			getAttrsFunc:        getAttrsFunc,
182			cache:               make([]*watchCacheEvent, defaultLowerBoundCapacity),
183			lowerBoundCapacity:  defaultLowerBoundCapacity,
184			upperBoundCapacity:  capacityUpperBound(eventFreshDuration),
185			startIndex:          0,
186			endIndex:            0,
187			store:               store.NewIndexer(indexers),
188			resourceVersion:     0,
189			listResourceVersion: 0,
190			eventHandler:        eventHandler,
191			clock:               clock,
192			eventFreshDuration:  eventFreshDuration,
193			versioner:           versioner,
194			groupResource:       groupResource,
195			waitingUntilFresh:   progressRequester,
196			getCurrentRV:        getCurrentRV,
197		}
198		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ListFromCacheSnapshot) {
199			wc.snapshottingEnabled.Store(true)
200			wc.snapshots = store.NewSnapshotter()
201		}
202		metrics.WatchCacheCapacity.WithLabelValues(groupResource.Group, groupResource.Resource).Set(float64(wc.capacity))
203		wc.cond = sync.NewCond(wc.RLocker())
204		wc.indexValidator = wc.isIndexValidLocked
205	
206		return wc
207	}
208	
209	// capacityUpperBound denotes the maximum possible capacity of the watch cache
210	// to which it can resize.
211	func capacityUpperBound(eventFreshDuration time.Duration) int {
212		if eventFreshDuration <= DefaultEventFreshDuration {
213			return defaultUpperBoundCapacity
214		}
215		// eventFreshDuration determines how long the watch events are supposed
216		// to be stored in the watch cache.
217		// In very high churn situations, there is a need to store more events
218		// in the watch cache, hence it would have to be upsized accordingly.
219		// Because of that, for larger values of eventFreshDuration, we set the
220		// upper bound of the watch cache's capacity proportionally to the ratio
221		// between eventFreshDuration and DefaultEventFreshDuration.
222		// Given that the watch cache size can only double, we round up that
223		// proportion to the next power of two.
224		exponent := int(math.Ceil((math.Log2(eventFreshDuration.Seconds() / DefaultEventFreshDuration.Seconds()))))
225		if maxExponent := int(math.Floor((math.Log2(math.MaxInt32 / defaultUpperBoundCapacity)))); exponent > maxExponent {
226			// Making sure that the capacity's upper bound fits in a 32-bit integer.
227			exponent = maxExponent
228			klog.Warningf("Capping watch cache capacity upper bound to %v", defaultUpperBoundCapacity<<exponent)
229		}
230		return defaultUpperBoundCapacity << exponent
231	}

233	// Add takes runtime.Object as an argument.
234	func (w *watchCache) Add(obj interface{}) error {
235		object, resourceVersion, err := w.objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj)
236		if err != nil {
237			return err
238		}
239		event := watch.Event{Type: watch.Added, Object: object}
240	
241		f := func(elem *store.Element) error { return w.store.Add(elem) }
242		return w.processEvent(event, resourceVersion, f)
243	}
244	
245	// Update takes runtime.Object as an argument.
246	func (w *watchCache) Update(obj interface{}) error {
247		object, resourceVersion, err := w.objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj)
248		if err != nil {
249			return err
250		}
251		event := watch.Event{Type: watch.Modified, Object: object}
252	
253		f := func(elem *store.Element) error { return w.store.Update(elem) }
254		return w.processEvent(event, resourceVersion, f)
255	}
256	
257	// Delete takes runtime.Object as an argument.
258	func (w *watchCache) Delete(obj interface{}) error {
259		object, resourceVersion, err := w.objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj)
260		if err != nil {
261			return err
262		}
263		event := watch.Event{Type: watch.Deleted, Object: object}
264	
265		f := func(elem *store.Element) error { return w.store.Delete(elem) }
266		return w.processEvent(event, resourceVersion, f)
267	}
268	
269	func (w *watchCache) objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj interface{}) (runtime.Object, uint64, error) {
270		object, ok := obj.(runtime.Object)
271		if !ok {
272			return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("obj does not implement runtime.Object interface: %v", obj)
273		}
274		resourceVersion, err := w.versioner.ObjectResourceVersion(object)
275		if err != nil {
276			return nil, 0, err
277		}
278		return object, resourceVersion, nil
279	}
280	
281	// processEvent is safe as long as there is at most one call to it in flight
282	// at any point in time.
283	func (w *watchCache) processEvent(event watch.Event, resourceVersion uint64, updateFunc func(*store.Element) error) error {
284		metrics.EventsReceivedCounter.WithLabelValues(w.groupResource.Group, w.groupResource.Resource).Inc()
285	
286		key, err := w.keyFunc(event.Object)
287		if err != nil {
288			return fmt.Errorf("couldn't compute key: %v", err)
289		}
290		elem := &store.Element{Key: key, Object: event.Object}
291		elem.Labels, elem.Fields, err = w.getAttrsFunc(event.Object)
292		if err != nil {
293			return err
294		}
295	
296		wcEvent := &watchCacheEvent{
297			Type:            event.Type,
298			Object:          elem.Object,
299			ObjLabels:       elem.Labels,
300			ObjFields:       elem.Fields,
301			Key:             key,
302			ResourceVersion: resourceVersion,
303			RecordTime:      w.clock.Now(),
304		}
305	
306		// We can call w.store.Get() outside of a critical section,
307		// because the w.store itself is thread-safe and the only
308		// place where w.store is modified is below (via updateFunc)
309		// and these calls are serialized because reflector is processing
310		// events one-by-one.
311		previous, exists, err := w.store.Get(elem)
312		if err != nil {
313			return err
314		}
315		if exists {
316			previousElem := previous.(*store.Element)
317			wcEvent.PrevObject = previousElem.Object
318			wcEvent.PrevObjLabels = previousElem.Labels
319			wcEvent.PrevObjFields = previousElem.Fields
320		}
321	
322		if err := func() error {
323			w.Lock()
324			defer w.Unlock()
325	
326			w.updateCache(wcEvent)
327			w.resourceVersion = resourceVersion
328			defer w.cond.Broadcast()
329	
330			err := updateFunc(elem)
331			if err != nil {
332				return err
333			}
334			if w.snapshots != nil && w.snapshottingEnabled.Load() {
335				if orderedLister, ordered := w.store.(store.OrderedLister); ordered {
336					if w.isCacheFullLocked() {
337						oldestRV := w.cache[w.startIndex%w.capacity].ResourceVersion
338						w.snapshots.RemoveLess(oldestRV)
339					}
340					w.snapshots.Add(w.resourceVersion, orderedLister)
341				}
342			}
343			return err
344		}(); err != nil {
345			return err
346		}
347	
348		// Avoid calling event handler under lock.
349		// This is safe as long as there is at most one call to Add/Update/Delete and
350		// UpdateResourceVersion in flight at any point in time, which is true now,
351		// because reflector calls them synchronously from its main thread.
352		if w.eventHandler != nil {
353			w.eventHandler(wcEvent)
354		}
355		metrics.RecordResourceVersion(w.groupResource, resourceVersion)
356		return nil
357	}
358	
359	// Assumes that lock is already held for write.
360	func (w *watchCache) updateCache(event *watchCacheEvent) {
361		w.resizeCacheLocked(event.RecordTime)
362		if w.isCacheFullLocked() {
363			// Cache is full - remove the oldest element.
364			w.startIndex++
365			w.removedEventSinceRelist = true
366		}
367		w.cache[w.endIndex%w.capacity] = event
368		w.endIndex++
369	}
370	
371	// resizeCacheLocked resizes the cache if necessary:
372	// - increases capacity by 2x if cache is full and all cached events occurred within last eventFreshDuration.
373	// - decreases capacity by 2x when recent quarter of events occurred outside of eventFreshDuration(protect watchCache from flapping).
374	func (w *watchCache) resizeCacheLocked(eventTime time.Time) {
375		if w.isCacheFullLocked() && eventTime.Sub(w.cache[w.startIndex%w.capacity].RecordTime) < w.eventFreshDuration {
376			capacity := min(w.capacity*2, w.upperBoundCapacity)
377			if capacity > w.capacity {
378				w.doCacheResizeLocked(capacity)
379			}
380			return
381		}
382		if w.isCacheFullLocked() && eventTime.Sub(w.cache[(w.endIndex-w.capacity/4)%w.capacity].RecordTime) > w.eventFreshDuration {
383			capacity := max(w.capacity/2, w.lowerBoundCapacity)
384			if capacity < w.capacity {
385				w.doCacheResizeLocked(capacity)
386			}
387			return
388		}
389	}
390	
391	// isCacheFullLocked used to judge whether watchCacheEvent is full.
392	// Assumes that lock is already held for write.
393	func (w *watchCache) isCacheFullLocked() bool {
394		return w.endIndex == w.startIndex+w.capacity
395	}
396	
397	// doCacheResizeLocked resize watchCache's event array with different capacity.
398	// Assumes that lock is already held for write.
399	func (w *watchCache) doCacheResizeLocked(capacity int) {
400		newCache := make([]*watchCacheEvent, capacity)
401		if capacity < w.capacity {
402			// adjust startIndex if cache capacity shrink.
403			w.startIndex = w.endIndex - capacity
404		}
405		for i := w.startIndex; i < w.endIndex; i++ {
406			newCache[i%capacity] = w.cache[i%w.capacity]
407		}
408		w.cache = newCache
409		metrics.RecordsWatchCacheCapacityChange(w.groupResource, w.capacity, capacity)
410		w.capacity = capacity
411	}
412	
413	func (w *watchCache) UpdateResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) {
414		rv, err := w.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion)
415		if err != nil {
416			klog.Errorf("Couldn't parse resourceVersion: %v", err)
417			return
418		}
419	
420		func() {
421			w.Lock()
422			defer w.Unlock()
423			w.resourceVersion = rv
424			w.cond.Broadcast()
425		}()
426	
427		// Avoid calling event handler under lock.
428		// This is safe as long as there is at most one call to Add/Update/Delete and
429		// UpdateResourceVersion in flight at any point in time, which is true now,
430		// because reflector calls them synchronously from its main thread.
431		if w.eventHandler != nil {
432			wcEvent := &watchCacheEvent{
433				Type:            watch.Bookmark,
434				ResourceVersion: rv,
435			}
436			w.eventHandler(wcEvent)
437		}
438		metrics.RecordResourceVersion(w.groupResource, rv)
439	}
440	
441	// List returns list of pointers to <store.Element> objects.
442	func (w *watchCache) List() []interface{} {
443		return w.store.List()
444	}
445	
446	// waitUntilFreshAndBlock waits until cache is at least as fresh as given <resourceVersion>.
447	// NOTE: This function acquired lock and doesn't release it.
448	// You HAVE TO explicitly call w.RUnlock() after this function.
449	func (w *watchCache) waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx context.Context, resourceVersion uint64) error {
450		startTime := w.clock.Now()
451		defer func() {
452			if resourceVersion > 0 {
453				metrics.WatchCacheReadWait.WithContext(ctx).WithLabelValues(w.groupResource.Group, w.groupResource.Resource).Observe(w.clock.Since(startTime).Seconds())
454			}
455		}()
456	
457		// In case resourceVersion is 0, we accept arbitrarily stale result.
458		// As a result, the condition in the below for loop will never be
459		// satisfied (w.resourceVersion is never negative), this call will
460		// never hit the w.cond.Wait().
461		// As a result - we can optimize the code by not firing the wakeup
462		// function (and avoid starting a gorotuine), especially given that
463		// resourceVersion=0 is the most common case.
464		if resourceVersion > 0 {
465			go func() {
466				// Wake us up when the time limit has expired.  The docs
467				// promise that time.After (well, NewTimer, which it calls)
468				// will wait *at least* the duration given. Since this go
469				// routine starts sometime after we record the start time, and
470				// it will wake up the loop below sometime after the broadcast,
471				// we don't need to worry about waking it up before the time
472				// has expired accidentally.
473				<-w.clock.After(blockTimeout)
474				w.cond.Broadcast()
475			}()
476		}
477	
478		w.RLock()
479		span := tracing.SpanFromContext(ctx)
480		span.AddEvent("watchCache locked acquired")
481		for w.resourceVersion < resourceVersion {
482			if w.clock.Since(startTime) >= blockTimeout {
483				// Request that the client retry after 'resourceVersionTooHighRetrySeconds' seconds.
484				return storage.NewTooLargeResourceVersionError(resourceVersion, w.resourceVersion, resourceVersionTooHighRetrySeconds)
485			}
486			w.cond.Wait()
487		}
488		span.AddEvent("watchCache fresh enough")
489		return nil
490	}

492	type sortableStoreElements []interface{}

494	func (s sortableStoreElements) Len() int {
495		return len(s)
496	}

498	func (s sortableStoreElements) Less(i, j int) bool {
499		return s[i].(*store.Element).Key < s[j].(*store.Element).Key
500	}

502	func (s sortableStoreElements) Swap(i, j int) {
503		s[i], s[j] = s[j], s[i]
504	}

506	func (c *watchCache) WaitUntilFreshAndGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions) (listResp, string, error) {
507		if opts.Recursive {
508			return c.waitUntilFreshAndList(ctx, key, opts)
509		}
510		return c.waitUntilFreshAndGetList(ctx, key, opts)
511	}

513	func (c *watchCache) waitUntilFreshAndGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions) (listResp, string, error) {
514		var listRV uint64
515		var err error
516		if opts.ResourceVersionMatch == "" && opts.ResourceVersion == "" {
517			// Consistent read
518			listRV, err = c.getCurrentRV(ctx)
519			if err != nil {
520				return listResp{}, "", err
521			}
522		} else {
523			listRV, err = c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)
524			if err != nil {
525				return listResp{}, "", err
526			}
527		}
528		obj, exists, readResourceVersion, err := c.WaitUntilFreshAndGet(ctx, listRV, key)
529		if err != nil {
530			return listResp{}, "", err
531		}
532		if exists {
533			return listResp{Items: []interface{}{obj}, ResourceVersion: readResourceVersion}, "", nil
534		}
535		return listResp{ResourceVersion: readResourceVersion}, "", nil
536	}

538	// WaitUntilFreshAndList returns list of pointers to `storeElement` objects along
539	// with their ResourceVersion and the name of the index, if any, that was used.
540	func (w *watchCache) WaitUntilFreshAndGetKeys(ctx context.Context, resourceVersion uint64) (keys []string, err error) {
541		if delegator.ConsistentReadSupported() && w.notFresh(resourceVersion) {
542			w.waitingUntilFresh.Add()
543			err = w.waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx, resourceVersion)
544			w.waitingUntilFresh.Remove()
545		} else {
546			err = w.waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx, resourceVersion)
547		}
548	
549		defer w.RUnlock()
550		if err != nil {
551			return nil, err
552		}
553		return w.store.ListKeys(), nil
554	}

556	// NOTICE: Structure follows the shouldDelegateList function in
557	// staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/delegator.go
558	func (w *watchCache) waitUntilFreshAndList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions) (resp listResp, index string, err error) {
559		listRV, err := w.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)
560		if err != nil {
561			return listResp{}, "", err
562		}
563		switch opts.ResourceVersionMatch {
564		case metav1.ResourceVersionMatchExact:
565			return w.waitAndListExactRV(ctx, key, "", listRV)
566		case metav1.ResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan:
567		case "":
568			// Continue
569			if len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {
570				continueKey, continueRV, err := storage.DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, key)
571				if err != nil {
572					return listResp{}, "", errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf("invalid continue token: %v", err))
573				}
574				if continueRV > 0 {
575					return w.waitAndListExactRV(ctx, key, continueKey, uint64(continueRV))
576				} else {
577					// Don't pass matchValues as they don't support continueKey
578					return w.waitAndListConsistent(ctx, key, continueKey, nil)
579				}
580			}
581			// Legacy exact match
582			if opts.Predicate.Limit > 0 && len(opts.ResourceVersion) > 0 && opts.ResourceVersion != "0" {
583				return w.waitAndListExactRV(ctx, key, "", listRV)
584			}
585			if opts.ResourceVersion == "" {
586				return w.waitAndListConsistent(ctx, key, "", opts.Predicate.MatcherIndex(ctx))
587			}
588		}
589		return w.waitAndListLatestRV(ctx, listRV, key, "", opts.Predicate.MatcherIndex(ctx))
590	}
591	
592	func (w *watchCache) waitAndListExactRV(ctx context.Context, key, continueKey string, resourceVersion uint64) (resp listResp, index string, err error) {
593		if delegator.ConsistentReadSupported() && w.notFresh(resourceVersion) {
594			w.waitingUntilFresh.Add()
595			err = w.waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx, resourceVersion)
596			w.waitingUntilFresh.Remove()
597		} else {
598			err = w.waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx, resourceVersion)
599		}
600		defer w.RUnlock()
601		if err != nil {
602			return listResp{}, "", err
603		}
604	
605		if w.snapshots == nil {
606			return listResp{}, "", errors.NewResourceExpired(fmt.Sprintf("too old resource version: %d", resourceVersion))
607		}
608		store, ok := w.snapshots.GetLessOrEqual(resourceVersion)
609		if !ok {
610			return listResp{}, "", errors.NewResourceExpired(fmt.Sprintf("too old resource version: %d", resourceVersion))
611		}
612		items := store.ListPrefix(key, continueKey)
613		return listResp{
614			Items:           items,
615			ResourceVersion: resourceVersion,
616		}, "", nil
617	}

619	func (w *watchCache) waitAndListConsistent(ctx context.Context, key, continueKey string, matchValues []storage.MatchValue) (resp listResp, index string, err error) {
620		resourceVersion, err := w.getCurrentRV(ctx)
621		if err != nil {
622			return listResp{}, "", err
623		}
624		return w.waitAndListLatestRV(ctx, resourceVersion, key, continueKey, matchValues)
625	}

627	func (w *watchCache) waitAndListLatestRV(ctx context.Context, resourceVersion uint64, key, continueKey string, matchValues []storage.MatchValue) (resp listResp, index string, err error) {
628		if delegator.ConsistentReadSupported() && w.notFresh(resourceVersion) {
629			w.waitingUntilFresh.Add()
630			err = w.waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx, resourceVersion)
631			w.waitingUntilFresh.Remove()
632		} else {
633			err = w.waitUntilFreshAndBlock(ctx, resourceVersion)
634		}
635		defer w.RUnlock()
636		if err != nil {
637			return listResp{}, "", err
638		}
639		return w.listLatestRV(key, continueKey, matchValues)
640	}

642	func (w *watchCache) listLatestRV(key, continueKey string, matchValues []storage.MatchValue) (resp listResp, index string, err error) {
643		// This isn't the place where we do "final filtering" - only some "prefiltering" is happening here. So the only
644		// requirement here is to NOT miss anything that should be returned. We can return as many non-matching items as we
645		// want - they will be filtered out later. The fact that we return less things is only further performance improvement.
646		// TODO: if multiple indexes match, return the one with the fewest items, so as to do as much filtering as possible.
647		for _, matchValue := range matchValues {
648			if result, err := w.store.ByIndex(matchValue.IndexName, matchValue.Value); err == nil {
649				result, err = filterPrefixAndOrder(key, result)
650				return listResp{
651					Items:           result,
652					ResourceVersion: w.resourceVersion,
653				}, matchValue.IndexName, err
654			}
655		}
656		if store, ok := w.store.(store.OrderedLister); ok {
657			result := store.ListPrefix(key, continueKey)
658			return listResp{
659				Items:           result,
660				ResourceVersion: w.resourceVersion,
661			}, "", nil
662		}
663		result := w.store.List()
664		result, err = filterPrefixAndOrder(key, result)
665		return listResp{
666			Items:           result,
667			ResourceVersion: w.resourceVersion,
668		}, "", err
669	}
670	
671	func filterPrefixAndOrder(prefix string, items []interface{}) ([]interface{}, error) {
672		var result []interface{}
673		for _, item := range items {
674			elem, ok := item.(*store.Element)
675			if !ok {
676				return nil, fmt.Errorf("non *store.Element returned from storage: %v", item)
677			}
678			if !hasPathPrefix(elem.Key, prefix) {
679				continue
680			}
681			result = append(result, item)
682		}
683		sort.Sort(sortableStoreElements(result))
684		return result, nil
685	}

687	func (w *watchCache) notFresh(resourceVersion uint64) bool {
688		w.RLock()
689		defer w.RUnlock()
690		return resourceVersion > w.resourceVersion
691	}

718		object, ok := obj.(runtime.Object)

725		}

735	// Replace takes slice of runtime.Object as a parameter.
736	func (w *watchCache) Replace(objs []interface{}, resourceVersion string) error {
737		version, err := w.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion)
738		if err != nil {
739			return err
740		}
741	
742		toReplace := make([]interface{}, 0, len(objs))
743		for _, obj := range objs {
744			object, ok := obj.(runtime.Object)
745			if !ok {
746				return fmt.Errorf("didn't get runtime.Object for replace: %#v", obj)
747			}
748			key, err := w.keyFunc(object)
749			if err != nil {
750				return fmt.Errorf("couldn't compute key: %v", err)
751			}
752			objLabels, objFields, err := w.getAttrsFunc(object)
753			if err != nil {
754				return err
755			}
756			toReplace = append(toReplace, &store.Element{
757				Key:    key,
758				Object: object,
759				Labels: objLabels,
760				Fields: objFields,
761			})
762		}
763	
764		w.Lock()
765		defer w.Unlock()
766	
767		// Ensure startIndex never decreases, so that existing watchCacheInterval
768		// instances get "invalid" errors if the try to download from the buffer
769		// using their own start/end indexes calculated from previous buffer
770		// content.
771	
772		// Empty the cyclic buffer, ensuring startIndex doesn't decrease.
773		w.startIndex = w.endIndex
774		w.removedEventSinceRelist = false
775	
776		if err := w.store.Replace(toReplace, resourceVersion); err != nil {
777			return err
778		}
779		if w.snapshots != nil {
780			w.snapshots.Reset()
781			if orderedLister, ordered := w.store.(store.OrderedLister); ordered && w.snapshottingEnabled.Load() {
782				w.snapshots.Add(version, orderedLister)
783			}
784		}
785		w.listResourceVersion = version
786		w.resourceVersion = version
787		if w.onReplace != nil {
788			w.onReplace()
789		}
790		w.cond.Broadcast()
791	
792		metrics.RecordResourceVersion(w.groupResource, version)
793		klog.V(3).Infof("Replaced watchCache (rev: %v) ", resourceVersion)
794		return nil
795	}

820	const (
821		// minWatchChanSize is the min size of channels used by the watch.
822		// We keep that set to 10 for "backward compatibility" until we
823		// convince ourselves based on some metrics that decreasing is safe.
824		minWatchChanSize = 10
825		// maxWatchChanSizeWithIndexAndTriger is the max size of the channel
826		// used by the watch using the index and trigger selector.
827		maxWatchChanSizeWithIndexAndTrigger = 10
828		// maxWatchChanSizeWithIndexWithoutTrigger is the max size of the channel
829		// used by the watch using the index but without triggering selector.
830		// We keep that set to 1000 for "backward compatibility", until we
831		// convinced ourselves based on some metrics that decreasing is safe.
832		maxWatchChanSizeWithIndexWithoutTrigger = 1000
833		// maxWatchChanSizeWithoutIndex is the max size of the channel
834		// used by the watch not using the index.
835		// TODO(wojtek-t): Figure out if the value shouldn't be higher.
836		maxWatchChanSizeWithoutIndex = 100
837	)
838	
839	func (w *watchCache) suggestedWatchChannelSize(indexExists, triggerUsed bool) int {
840		// To estimate the channel size we use a heuristic that a channel
841		// should roughly be able to keep one second of history.
842		// We don't have an exact data, but given we store updates from
843		// the last <eventFreshDuration>, we approach it by dividing the
844		// capacity by the length of the history window.
845		chanSize := int(math.Ceil(float64(w.currentCapacity()) / w.eventFreshDuration.Seconds()))
846	
847		// Finally we adjust the size to avoid ending with too low or
848		// to large values.
849		if chanSize < minWatchChanSize {
850			chanSize = minWatchChanSize
851		}
852		var maxChanSize int
853		switch {
854		case indexExists && triggerUsed:
855			maxChanSize = maxWatchChanSizeWithIndexAndTrigger
856		case indexExists && !triggerUsed:
857			maxChanSize = maxWatchChanSizeWithIndexWithoutTrigger
858		case !indexExists:
859			maxChanSize = maxWatchChanSizeWithoutIndex
860		}
861		if chanSize > maxChanSize {
862			chanSize = maxChanSize
863		}
864		return chanSize
865	}

867	// isIndexValidLocked checks if a given index is still valid.
868	// This assumes that the lock is held.
869	func (w *watchCache) isIndexValidLocked(index int) bool {
870		return index >= w.startIndex
871	}

873	// getAllEventsSinceLocked returns a watchCacheInterval that can be used to
874	// retrieve events since a certain resourceVersion. This function assumes to
875	// be called under the watchCache lock.
876	func (w *watchCache) getAllEventsSinceLocked(resourceVersion uint64, key string, opts storage.ListOptions) (*watchCacheInterval, error) {
877		_, matchesSingle := opts.Predicate.MatchesSingle()
878		matchesSingle = matchesSingle && !opts.Recursive
879		if opts.SendInitialEvents != nil && *opts.SendInitialEvents {
880			return w.getIntervalFromStoreLocked(key, matchesSingle)
881		}
882	
883		size := w.endIndex - w.startIndex
884		var oldest uint64
885		switch {
886		case w.listResourceVersion > 0 && !w.removedEventSinceRelist:
887			// If no event was removed from the buffer since last relist, the oldest watch
888			// event we can deliver is one greater than the resource version of the list.
889			oldest = w.listResourceVersion + 1
890		case size > 0:
891			// If the previous condition is not satisfied: either some event was already
892			// removed from the buffer or we've never completed a list (the latter can
893			// only happen in unit tests that populate the buffer without performing
894			// list/replace operations), the oldest watch event we can deliver is the first
895			// one in the buffer.
896			oldest = w.cache[w.startIndex%w.capacity].ResourceVersion
897		default:
898			return nil, fmt.Errorf("watch cache isn't correctly initialized")
899		}
900	
901		if resourceVersion == 0 {
902			if opts.SendInitialEvents == nil {
903				// resourceVersion = 0 means that we don't require any specific starting point
904				// and we would like to start watching from ~now.
905				// However, to keep backward compatibility, we additionally need to return the
906				// current state and only then start watching from that point.
907				//
908				// TODO: In v2 api, we should stop returning the current state - #13969.
909				return w.getIntervalFromStoreLocked(key, matchesSingle)
910			}
911			// SendInitialEvents = false and resourceVersion = 0
912			// means that the request would like to start watching
913			// from Any resourceVersion
914			resourceVersion = w.resourceVersion
915		}
916		if resourceVersion < oldest-1 {
917			return nil, errors.NewResourceExpired(fmt.Sprintf("too old resource version: %d (%d)", resourceVersion, oldest-1))
918		}
919	
920		// Binary search the smallest index at which resourceVersion is greater than the given one.
921		f := func(i int) bool {
922			return w.cache[(w.startIndex+i)%w.capacity].ResourceVersion > resourceVersion
923		}
924		first := sort.Search(size, f)
925		indexerFunc := func(i int) *watchCacheEvent {
926			return w.cache[i%w.capacity]
927		}
928		ci := newCacheInterval(w.startIndex+first, w.endIndex, indexerFunc, w.indexValidator, resourceVersion, w.RWMutex.RLocker())
929		return ci, nil
930	}
931	
932	// getIntervalFromStoreLocked returns a watchCacheInterval
933	// that covers the entire storage state.
934	// This function assumes to be called under the watchCache lock.
935	func (w *watchCache) getIntervalFromStoreLocked(key string, matchesSingle bool) (*watchCacheInterval, error) {
936		ci, err := newCacheIntervalFromStore(w.resourceVersion, w.store, key, matchesSingle)
937		if err != nil {
938			return nil, err
939		}
940		return ci, nil
941	}

943	func (w *watchCache) Compact(rev uint64) {
944		if w.snapshots == nil {
945			return
946		}
947		w.snapshots.RemoveLess(rev)
948	}

950	func (w *watchCache) MarkConsistent(consistent bool) {
951		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ListFromCacheSnapshot) {
952			w.snapshottingEnabled.Store(consistent)
953			if !consistent && w.snapshots != nil {
954				w.snapshots.Reset()
955			}
956		}

70		// We set it to defaultBookmarkFrequency plus epsilon to maximize
71		// chances that last bookmark was sent within kept history, at the
72		// same time, minimizing the needed memory usage.
73		DefaultEventFreshDuration = defaultBookmarkFrequency + 15*time.Second
74	
75		// defaultBookmarkFrequency defines how frequently watch bookmarks should be send
76		// in addition to sending a bookmark right before watch deadline.
77		defaultBookmarkFrequency = time.Minute
78	)

126	type watchersMap map[int]*cacheWatcher
127	
128	func (wm watchersMap) addWatcher(w *cacheWatcher, number int) {
129		wm[number] = w
130	}
131	
132	func (wm watchersMap) deleteWatcher(number int) {
133		delete(wm, number)
134	}

136	func (wm watchersMap) terminateAll(done func(*cacheWatcher)) {
137		for key, watcher := range wm {
138			delete(wm, key)
139			done(watcher)
140		}
141	}

143	type indexedWatchers struct {
144		allWatchers   map[namespacedName]watchersMap
145		valueWatchers map[string]watchersMap
146	}
147	
148	func (i *indexedWatchers) addWatcher(w *cacheWatcher, number int, scope namespacedName, value string, supported bool) {
149		if supported {
150			if _, ok := i.valueWatchers[value]; !ok {
151				i.valueWatchers[value] = watchersMap{}
152			}
153			i.valueWatchers[value].addWatcher(w, number)
154		} else {
155			scopedWatchers, ok := i.allWatchers[scope]
156			if !ok {
157				scopedWatchers = watchersMap{}
158				i.allWatchers[scope] = scopedWatchers
159			}
160			scopedWatchers.addWatcher(w, number)
161		}
162	}
163	
164	func (i *indexedWatchers) deleteWatcher(number int, scope namespacedName, value string, supported bool) {
165		if supported {
166			i.valueWatchers[value].deleteWatcher(number)
167			if len(i.valueWatchers[value]) == 0 {
168				delete(i.valueWatchers, value)
169			}
170		} else {
171			i.allWatchers[scope].deleteWatcher(number)
172			if len(i.allWatchers[scope]) == 0 {
173				delete(i.allWatchers, scope)
174			}
175		}
176	}
177	
178	func (i *indexedWatchers) terminateAll(groupResource schema.GroupResource, done func(*cacheWatcher)) {
179		// note that we don't have to call setDrainInputBufferLocked method on the watchers
180		// because we take advantage of the default value - stop immediately
181		// also watchers that have had already its draining strategy set
182		// are no longer available (they were removed from the allWatchers and the valueWatchers maps)
183		if len(i.allWatchers) > 0 || len(i.valueWatchers) > 0 {
184			klog.Warningf("Terminating all watchers from cacher %v", groupResource)
185		}
186		for _, watchers := range i.allWatchers {
187			watchers.terminateAll(done)
188		}
189		for _, watchers := range i.valueWatchers {
190			watchers.terminateAll(done)
191		}
192		i.allWatchers = map[namespacedName]watchersMap{}
193		i.valueWatchers = map[string]watchersMap{}
194	}

196	// As we don't need a high precision here, we keep all watchers timeout within a
197	// second in a bucket, and pop up them once at the timeout. To be more specific,
198	// if you set fire time at X, you can get the bookmark within (X-1,X+1) period.
199	type watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets struct {
200		// the key of watcherBuckets is the number of seconds since createTime
201		watchersBuckets   map[int64][]*cacheWatcher
202		createTime        time.Time
203		startBucketID     int64
204		clock             clock.Clock
205		bookmarkFrequency time.Duration
206	}
207	
208	func newTimeBucketWatchers(clock clock.Clock, bookmarkFrequency time.Duration) *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets {
209		return &watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets{
210			watchersBuckets:   make(map[int64][]*cacheWatcher),
211			createTime:        clock.Now(),
212			startBucketID:     0,
213			clock:             clock,
214			bookmarkFrequency: bookmarkFrequency,
215		}
216	}
217	
218	// adds a watcher to the bucket, if the deadline is before the start, it will be
219	// added to the first one.
220	func (t *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets) addWatcherThreadUnsafe(w *cacheWatcher) bool {
221		// note that the returned time can be before t.createTime,
222		// especially in cases when the nextBookmarkTime method
223		// give us the zero value of type Time
224		// so buckedID can hold a negative value
225		nextTime, ok := w.nextBookmarkTime(t.clock.Now(), t.bookmarkFrequency)
226		if !ok {
227			return false
228		}
229		bucketID := int64(nextTime.Sub(t.createTime) / time.Second)
230		if bucketID < t.startBucketID {
231			bucketID = t.startBucketID
232		}
233		watchers := t.watchersBuckets[bucketID]
234		t.watchersBuckets[bucketID] = append(watchers, w)
235		return true
236	}
237	
238	func (t *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets) popExpiredWatchersThreadUnsafe() [][]*cacheWatcher {
239		currentBucketID := int64(t.clock.Since(t.createTime) / time.Second)
240		// There should be one or two elements in almost all cases
241		expiredWatchers := make([][]*cacheWatcher, 0, 2)
242		for ; t.startBucketID <= currentBucketID; t.startBucketID++ {
243			if watchers, ok := t.watchersBuckets[t.startBucketID]; ok {
244				delete(t.watchersBuckets, t.startBucketID)
245				expiredWatchers = append(expiredWatchers, watchers)
246			}
247		}
248		return expiredWatchers
249	}

258	// Cacher is responsible for serving WATCH and LIST requests for a given
259	// resource from its internal cache and updating its cache in the background
260	// based on the underlying storage contents.
261	// Cacher implements storage.Interface (although most of the calls are just
262	// delegated to the underlying storage).
263	type Cacher struct {
264		// HighWaterMarks for performance debugging.
265		// Important: Since HighWaterMark is using sync/atomic, it has to be at the top of the struct due to a bug on 32-bit platforms
266		// See: https://golang.org/pkg/sync/atomic/ for more information
267		incomingHWM storage.HighWaterMark
268		// Incoming events that should be dispatched to watchers.
269		incoming chan watchCacheEvent
270	
271		resourcePrefix string
272	
273		sync.RWMutex
274	
275		// Before accessing the cacher's cache, wait for the ready to be ok.
276		// This is necessary to prevent users from accessing structures that are
277		// uninitialized or are being repopulated right now.
278		// ready needs to be set to false when the cacher is paused or stopped.
279		// ready needs to be set to true when the cacher is ready to use after
280		// initialization.
281		ready *ready
282	
283		// Underlying storage.Interface.
284		storage storage.Interface
285	
286		// Expected type of objects in the underlying cache.
287		objectType reflect.Type
288		// Used for logging, to disambiguate *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs)
289		groupResource schema.GroupResource
290	
291		// "sliding window" of recent changes of objects and the current state.
292		watchCache *watchCache
293		reflector  *cache.Reflector
294	
295		// Versioner is used to handle resource versions.
296		versioner storage.Versioner
297	
298		// newFunc is a function that creates new empty object storing a object of type Type.
299		newFunc func() runtime.Object
300	
301		// newListFunc is a function that creates new empty list for storing objects of type Type.
302		newListFunc func() runtime.Object
303	
304		// indexedTrigger is used for optimizing amount of watchers that needs to process
305		// an incoming event.
306		indexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc
307		// watchers is mapping from the value of trigger function that a
308		// watcher is interested into the watchers
309		watcherIdx int
310		watchers   indexedWatchers
311	
312		// Defines a time budget that can be spend on waiting for not-ready watchers
313		// while dispatching event before shutting them down.
314		dispatchTimeoutBudget timeBudget
315	
316		// Handling graceful termination.
317		stopLock sync.RWMutex
318		stopped  bool
319		stopCh   chan struct{}
320		stopWg   sync.WaitGroup
321	
322		clock clock.Clock
323		// timer is used to avoid unnecessary allocations in underlying watchers.
324		timer *time.Timer
325	
326		// dispatching determines whether there is currently dispatching of
327		// any event in flight.
328		dispatching bool
329		// watchersBuffer is a list of watchers potentially interested in currently
330		// dispatched event.
331		watchersBuffer []*cacheWatcher
332		// blockedWatchers is a list of watchers whose buffer is currently full.
333		blockedWatchers []*cacheWatcher
334		// watchersToStop is a list of watchers that were supposed to be stopped
335		// during current dispatching, but stopping was deferred to the end of
336		// dispatching that event to avoid race with closing channels in watchers.
337		watchersToStop []*cacheWatcher
338		// Maintain a timeout queue to send the bookmark event before the watcher times out.
339		// Note that this field when accessed MUST be protected by the Cacher.lock.
340		bookmarkWatchers *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets
341		// expiredBookmarkWatchers is a list of watchers that were expired and need to be schedule for a next bookmark event
342		expiredBookmarkWatchers []*cacheWatcher
343		compactor               *compactor
344	}
345	
346	// NewCacherFromConfig creates a new Cacher responsible for servicing WATCH and LIST requests from
347	// its internal cache and updating its cache in the background based on the
348	// given configuration.
349	func NewCacherFromConfig(config Config) (*Cacher, error) {
350		stopCh := make(chan struct{})
351		obj := config.NewFunc()
352		// Give this error when it is constructed rather than when you get the
353		// first watch item, because it's much easier to track down that way.
354		if err := runtime.CheckCodec(config.Codec, obj); err != nil {
355			return nil, fmt.Errorf("storage codec doesn't seem to match given type: %v", err)
356		}
357	
358		var indexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc
359		if config.IndexerFuncs != nil {
360			// For now, we don't support multiple trigger functions defined
361			// for a given resource.
362			if len(config.IndexerFuncs) > 1 {
363				return nil, fmt.Errorf("cacher %s doesn't support more than one IndexerFunc: ", reflect.TypeOf(obj).String())
364			}
365			for key, value := range config.IndexerFuncs {
366				if value != nil {
367					indexedTrigger = &indexedTriggerFunc{
368						indexName:   key,
369						indexerFunc: value,
370					}
371				}
372			}
373		}
374	
375		if config.Clock == nil {
376			config.Clock = clock.RealClock{}
377		}
378		objType := reflect.TypeOf(obj)
379		resourcePrefix := config.ResourcePrefix
380		if resourcePrefix == "" {
381			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resourcePrefix cannot be empty")
382		}
383		if resourcePrefix == "/" {
384			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resourcePrefix cannot be /")
385		}
386		if !strings.HasPrefix(resourcePrefix, "/") {
387			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resourcePrefix needs to start from /")
388		}
389		cacher := &Cacher{
390			resourcePrefix: resourcePrefix,
391			ready:          newReady(config.Clock),
392			storage:        config.Storage,
393			objectType:     objType,
394			groupResource:  config.GroupResource,
395			versioner:      config.Versioner,
396			newFunc:        config.NewFunc,
397			newListFunc:    config.NewListFunc,
398			indexedTrigger: indexedTrigger,
399			watcherIdx:     0,
400			watchers: indexedWatchers{
401				allWatchers:   make(map[namespacedName]watchersMap),
402				valueWatchers: make(map[string]watchersMap),
403			},
404			// TODO: Figure out the correct value for the buffer size.
405			incoming:              make(chan watchCacheEvent, 100),
406			dispatchTimeoutBudget: newTimeBudget(),
407			// We need to (potentially) stop both:
408			// - wait.Until go-routine
409			// - reflector.ListAndWatch
410			// and there are no guarantees on the order that they will stop.
411			// So we will be simply closing the channel, and synchronizing on the WaitGroup.
412			stopCh:           stopCh,
413			clock:            config.Clock,
414			timer:            time.NewTimer(time.Duration(0)),
415			bookmarkWatchers: newTimeBucketWatchers(config.Clock, defaultBookmarkFrequency),
416		}
417	
418		// Ensure that timer is stopped.
419		if !cacher.timer.Stop() {
420			// Consume triggered (but not yet received) timer event
421			// so that future reuse does not get a spurious timeout.
422			<-cacher.timer.C
423		}
424		var contextMetadata metadata.MD
425		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SeparateCacheWatchRPC) {
426			// Add grpc context metadata to watch and progress notify requests done by cacher to:
427			// * Prevent starvation of watch opened by cacher, by moving it to separate Watch RPC than watch request that bypass cacher.
428			// * Ensure that progress notification requests are executed on the same Watch RPC as their watch, which is required for it to work.
429			contextMetadata = metadata.New(map[string]string{"source": "cache"})
430		}
431	
432		eventFreshDuration := config.EventsHistoryWindow
433		if eventFreshDuration < DefaultEventFreshDuration {
434			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config.EventsHistoryWindow (%v) must not be below %v", eventFreshDuration, DefaultEventFreshDuration)
435		}
436	
437		progressRequester := progress.NewConditionalProgressRequester(config.Storage.RequestWatchProgress, config.Clock, contextMetadata)
438		watchCache := newWatchCache(
439			config.KeyFunc, cacher.processEvent, config.GetAttrsFunc, config.Versioner, config.Indexers,
440			config.Clock, eventFreshDuration, config.GroupResource, progressRequester, config.Storage.GetCurrentResourceVersion)
441		listerWatcher := NewListerWatcher(config.Storage, resourcePrefix, config.NewListFunc, contextMetadata)
442		reflectorName := "storage/cacher.go:" + resourcePrefix
443	
444		reflector := cache.NewNamedReflector(reflectorName, listerWatcher, obj, watchCache, 0)
445		// Configure reflector's pager to for an appropriate pagination chunk size for fetching data from
446		// storage. The pager falls back to full list if paginated list calls fail due to an "Expired" error.
447		reflector.WatchListPageSize = storageWatchListPageSize
448		// When etcd loses leader for 3 cycles, it returns error "no leader".
449		// We don't want to terminate all watchers as recreating all watchers puts high load on api-server.
450		// In most of the cases, leader is reelected within few cycles.
451		reflector.MaxInternalErrorRetryDuration = time.Second * 30
452	
453		cacher.watchCache = watchCache
454		cacher.reflector = reflector
455	
456		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SizeBasedListCostEstimate) {
457			err := config.Storage.EnableResourceSizeEstimation(cacher.getKeys)
458			if err != nil {
459				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to enable resource size estimation: %w", err)
460			}
461		}
462	
463		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ListFromCacheSnapshot) {
464			cacher.compactor = newCompactor(config.Storage, watchCache, config.Clock)
465			go cacher.compactor.Run(stopCh)
466		}
467	
468		go cacher.dispatchEvents()
469		go progressRequester.Run(stopCh)
470	
471		cacher.stopWg.Add(1)
472		go func() {
473			defer cacher.stopWg.Done()
474			defer cacher.terminateAllWatchers()
475			wait.Until(
476				func() {
477					if !cacher.isStopped() {
478						cacher.startCaching(stopCh)
479					}
480				}, time.Second, stopCh,
481			)
482		}()
483		return cacher, nil
484	}
485	
486	func (c *Cacher) startCaching(stopChannel <-chan struct{}) {
487		c.watchCache.SetOnReplace(func() {
488			c.ready.setReady()
489			klog.V(1).InfoS("cacher initialized", "group", c.groupResource.Group, "resource", c.groupResource.Resource)
490			metrics.WatchCacheInitializations.WithLabelValues(c.groupResource.Group, c.groupResource.Resource).Inc()
491		})
492		var err error
493		defer func() {
494			c.ready.setError(err)
495		}()
496	
497		c.terminateAllWatchers()
498		err = c.reflector.ListAndWatch(stopChannel)
499		if err != nil {
500			klog.Errorf("cacher (%v): unexpected ListAndWatch error: %v; reinitializing...", c.groupResource.String(), err)
501		}
502	}
503	
504	type namespacedName struct {
505		namespace string
506		name      string
507	}
508	
509	func (c *Cacher) Watch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {
510		ctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, "cacher.Watch",
511			attribute.String("audit-id", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(ctx)),
512			attribute.Stringer("type", c.groupResource))
513		defer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)
514		key, err := c.prepareKey(key, opts.Recursive)
515		if err != nil {
516			return nil, err
517		}
518		pred := opts.Predicate
519		requestedWatchRV, err := c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)
520		if err != nil {
521			return nil, err
522		}
523	
524		var readyGeneration int
525		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ResilientWatchCacheInitialization) {
526			var err error
527			var downtime time.Duration
528			readyGeneration, downtime, err = c.ready.checkAndReadGeneration()
529			if err != nil {
530				return nil, errors.NewTooManyRequests(err.Error(), calculateRetryAfterForUnreadyCache(downtime))
531			}
532		} else {
533			readyGeneration, err = c.ready.waitAndReadGeneration(ctx)
534			if err != nil {
535				return nil, errors.NewServiceUnavailable(err.Error())
536			}
537		}
538	
539		// determine the namespace and name scope of the watch, first from the request, secondarily from the field selector
540		scope := namespacedName{}
541		if requestNamespace, ok := request.NamespaceFrom(ctx); ok && len(requestNamespace) > 0 {
542			scope.namespace = requestNamespace
543		} else if selectorNamespace, ok := pred.Field.RequiresExactMatch("metadata.namespace"); ok {
544			scope.namespace = selectorNamespace
545		}
546		if requestInfo, ok := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx); ok && requestInfo != nil && len(requestInfo.Name) > 0 {
547			scope.name = requestInfo.Name
548		} else if selectorName, ok := pred.Field.RequiresExactMatch("metadata.name"); ok {
549			scope.name = selectorName
550		}
551	
552		// for request like '/api/v1/watch/namespaces/*', set scope.namespace to empty.
553		// namespaces don't populate metadata.namespace in ObjFields.
554		if c.groupResource == coreNamespaceResource && len(scope.namespace) > 0 && scope.namespace == scope.name {
555			scope.namespace = ""
556		}
557	
558		triggerValue, triggerSupported := "", false
559		if c.indexedTrigger != nil {
560			for _, field := range pred.IndexFields {
561				if field == c.indexedTrigger.indexName {
562					if value, ok := pred.Field.RequiresExactMatch(field); ok {
563						triggerValue, triggerSupported = value, true
564						break
565					}
566				}
567			}
568		}
569	
570		// It boils down to a tradeoff between:
571		// - having it as small as possible to reduce memory usage
572		// - having it large enough to ensure that watchers that need to process
573		//   a bunch of changes have enough buffer to avoid from blocking other
574		//   watchers on our watcher having a processing hiccup
575		chanSize := c.watchCache.suggestedWatchChannelSize(c.indexedTrigger != nil, triggerSupported)
576	
577		// client-go is going to fall back to a standard LIST on any error
578		// returned for watch-list requests
579		if isListWatchRequest(opts) && !etcdfeature.DefaultFeatureSupportChecker.Supports(storage.RequestWatchProgress) {
580			return newErrWatcher(fmt.Errorf("a watch stream was requested by the client but the required storage feature %s is disabled", storage.RequestWatchProgress)), nil
581		}
582	
583		// Determine the ResourceVersion to which the watch cache must be synchronized
584		requiredResourceVersion, err := c.getWatchCacheResourceVersion(ctx, requestedWatchRV, opts)
585		if err != nil {
586			return newErrWatcher(err), nil
587		}
588	
589		// Determine a function that computes the bookmarkAfterResourceVersion
590		bookmarkAfterResourceVersionFn, err := c.getBookmarkAfterResourceVersionLockedFunc(requestedWatchRV, requiredResourceVersion, opts)
591		if err != nil {
592			return newErrWatcher(err), nil
593		}
594	
595		// Determine watch timeout('0' means deadline is not set, ignore checking)
596		deadline, _ := ctx.Deadline()
597	
598		identifier := fmt.Sprintf("key: %q, labels: %q, fields: %q", key, pred.Label, pred.Field)
599	
600		// Create a watcher here to reduce memory allocations under lock,
601		// given that memory allocation may trigger GC and block the thread.
602		// Also note that emptyFunc is a placeholder, until we will be able
603		// to compute watcher.forget function (which has to happen under lock).
604		watcher := newCacheWatcher(
605			chanSize,
606			filterWithAttrsAndPrefixFunction(key, pred, c.groupResource),
607			emptyFunc,
608			c.versioner,
609			deadline,
610			pred.AllowWatchBookmarks,
611			c.groupResource,
612			identifier,
613		)
614	
615		// note that c.waitUntilWatchCacheFreshAndForceAllEvents must be called without
616		// the c.watchCache.RLock held otherwise we are at risk of a deadlock
617		// mainly because c.watchCache.processEvent method won't be able to make progress
618		//
619		// moreover even though the c.waitUntilWatchCacheFreshAndForceAllEvents acquires a lock
620		// it is safe to release the lock after the method finishes because we don't require
621		// any atomicity between the call to the method and further calls that actually get the events.
622		err = c.waitUntilWatchCacheFreshAndForceAllEvents(ctx, requiredResourceVersion, opts)
623		if err != nil {
624			return newErrWatcher(err), nil
625		}
626	
627		// We explicitly use thread unsafe version and do locking ourself to ensure that
628		// no new events will be processed in the meantime. The watchCache will be unlocked
629		// on return from this function.
630		// Note that we cannot do it under Cacher lock, to avoid a deadlock, since the
631		// underlying watchCache is calling processEvent under its lock.
632		c.watchCache.RLock()
633		defer c.watchCache.RUnlock()
634	
635		var cacheInterval *watchCacheInterval
636		cacheInterval, err = c.watchCache.getAllEventsSinceLocked(requiredResourceVersion, key, opts)
637		if err != nil {
638			// To match the uncached watch implementation, once we have passed authn/authz/admission,
639			// and successfully parsed a resource version, other errors must fail with a watch event of type ERROR,
640			// rather than a directly returned error.
641			return newErrWatcher(err), nil
642		}
643	
644		c.setInitialEventsEndBookmarkIfRequested(cacheInterval, opts, c.watchCache.resourceVersion)
645	
646		addedWatcher := false
647		func() {
648			c.Lock()
649			defer c.Unlock()
650	
651			if generation, _, err := c.ready.checkAndReadGeneration(); generation != readyGeneration || err != nil {
652				// We went unready or are already on a different generation.
653				// Avoid registering and starting the watch as it will have to be
654				// terminated immediately anyway.
655				return
656			}
657	
658			// Update watcher.forget function once we can compute it.
659			watcher.forget = forgetWatcher(c, watcher, c.watcherIdx, scope, triggerValue, triggerSupported)
660			// Update the bookMarkAfterResourceVersion
661			watcher.setBookmarkAfterResourceVersion(bookmarkAfterResourceVersionFn())
662			c.watchers.addWatcher(watcher, c.watcherIdx, scope, triggerValue, triggerSupported)
663			addedWatcher = true
664	
665			// Add it to the queue only when the client support watch bookmarks.
666			if watcher.allowWatchBookmarks {
667				c.bookmarkWatchers.addWatcherThreadUnsafe(watcher)
668			}
669			c.watcherIdx++
670		}()
671	
672		if !addedWatcher {
673			// Watcher isn't really started at this point, so it's safe to just drop it.
674			//
675			// We're simulating the immediate watch termination, which boils down to simply
676			// closing the watcher.
677			return newImmediateCloseWatcher(), nil
678		}
679	
680		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ShardedListAndWatch) && pred.ShardSelector != nil && !pred.ShardSelector.Empty() {
681			metrics.RecordShardedWatchStarted(c.groupResource)
682			originalForget := watcher.forget
683			watcher.forget = func(drainWatcher bool) {
684				metrics.RecordShardedWatchStopped(c.groupResource)
685				originalForget(drainWatcher)
686			}
687		}
688	
689		go watcher.processInterval(ctx, cacheInterval, requiredResourceVersion)
690		return watcher, nil
691	}

693	func (c *Cacher) Get(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error {
694		key, err := c.prepareKey(key, false)
695		if err != nil {
696			return err
697		}
698		getRV, err := c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)
699		if err != nil {
700			return err
701		}
702	
703		objVal, err := conversion.EnforcePtr(objPtr)
704		if err != nil {
705			return err
706		}
707	
708		obj, exists, readResourceVersion, err := c.watchCache.WaitUntilFreshAndGet(ctx, getRV, key)
709		if err != nil {
710			return err
711		}
712	
713		if exists {
714			elem, ok := obj.(*store.Element)
715			if !ok {
716				return fmt.Errorf("non *store.Element returned from storage: %v", obj)
717			}
718			objVal.Set(reflect.ValueOf(elem.Object).Elem())
719		} else {
720			objVal.Set(reflect.Zero(objVal.Type()))
721			if !opts.IgnoreNotFound {
722				return storage.NewKeyNotFoundError(key, int64(readResourceVersion))
723			}
724		}
725		return nil
726	}

728	// computeListLimit determines whether the cacher should
729	// apply a limit to an incoming LIST request and returns its value.
730	//
731	// note that this function doesn't check RVM nor the Continuation token.
732	// these parameters are validated by the shouldDelegateList function.
733	//
734	// as of today, the limit is ignored for requests that set RV == 0
735	func computeListLimit(opts storage.ListOptions) int64 {
736		if opts.Predicate.Limit <= 0 || opts.ResourceVersion == "0" {
737			return 0
738		}
739		return opts.Predicate.Limit
740	}

744		ResourceVersion uint64

747	// GetList implements storage.Interface
748	func (c *Cacher) GetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error {
749		preparedKey, err := c.prepareKey(key, opts.Recursive)
750		if err != nil {
751			return err
752		}
753		_, err = c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)
754		if err != nil {
755			return err
756		}
757	
758		ctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, "cacher.GetList",
759			attribute.String("audit-id", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(ctx)),
760			attribute.Stringer("type", c.groupResource))
761		defer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)
762	
763		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ResilientWatchCacheInitialization) {
764			if downtime, err := c.ready.check(); err != nil {
765				// If Cacher is not initialized, reject List requests
766				// as described in https://kep.k8s.io/4568
767				return errors.NewTooManyRequests(err.Error(), calculateRetryAfterForUnreadyCache(downtime))
768			}
769		} else {
770			if err := c.ready.wait(ctx); err != nil {
771				return errors.NewServiceUnavailable(err.Error())
772			}
773		}
774		span.AddEvent("Ready")
775	
776		// List elements with at least 'listRV' from cache.
777		listPtr, err := meta.GetItemsPtr(listObj)
778		if err != nil {
779			return err
780		}
781		listVal, err := conversion.EnforcePtr(listPtr)
782		if err != nil {
783			return err
784		}
785		if listVal.Kind() != reflect.Slice {
786			return fmt.Errorf("need a pointer to slice, got %v", listVal.Kind())
787		}
788	
789		resp, indexUsed, err := c.watchCache.WaitUntilFreshAndGetList(ctx, preparedKey, opts)
790		if err != nil {
791			return err
792		}
793		span.AddEvent("Listed items from cache", attribute.Int("count", len(resp.Items)))
794		// store pointer of eligible objects,
795		// Why not directly put object in the items of listObj?
796		//   the elements in ListObject are Struct type, making slice will bring excessive memory consumption.
797		//   so we try to delay this action as much as possible
798		var selectedObjects []runtime.Object
799		var lastSelectedObjectKey string
800		var hasMoreListItems bool
801		limit := computeListLimit(opts)
802		for i, obj := range resp.Items {
803			elem, ok := obj.(*store.Element)
804			if !ok {
805				return fmt.Errorf("non *store.Element returned from storage: %v", obj)
806			}
807			shardMatch := true
808			if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ShardedListAndWatch) {
809				var err error
810				shardMatch, err = opts.Predicate.MatchesSharding(elem.Object)
811				if err != nil {
812					return fmt.Errorf("shard matching failed: %w", err)
813				}
814			}
815			if shardMatch && opts.Predicate.MatchesObjectAttributes(elem.Labels, elem.Fields) {
816				selectedObjects = append(selectedObjects, elem.Object)
817				lastSelectedObjectKey = elem.Key
818			}
819			if limit > 0 && int64(len(selectedObjects)) >= limit {
820				hasMoreListItems = i < len(resp.Items)-1
821				break
822			}
823		}
824		if len(selectedObjects) == 0 {
825			// Ensure that we never return a nil Items pointer in the result for consistency.
826			listVal.Set(reflect.MakeSlice(listVal.Type(), 0, 0))
827		} else {
828			// Resize the slice appropriately, since we already know that size of result set
829			listVal.Set(reflect.MakeSlice(listVal.Type(), len(selectedObjects), len(selectedObjects)))
830			span.AddEvent("Resized result")
831			for i, o := range selectedObjects {
832				listVal.Index(i).Set(reflect.ValueOf(o).Elem())
833			}
834		}
835		span.AddEvent("Filtered items", attribute.Int("count", listVal.Len()))
836		if c.versioner != nil {
837			continueValue, remainingItemCount, err := storage.PrepareContinueToken(lastSelectedObjectKey, key, int64(resp.ResourceVersion), int64(len(resp.Items)), hasMoreListItems, opts)
838			if err != nil {
839				return err
840			}
841	
842			if err = c.versioner.UpdateList(listObj, resp.ResourceVersion, continueValue, remainingItemCount); err != nil {
843				return err
844			}
845		}
846		if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ShardedListAndWatch) {
847			opts.Predicate.SetShardInfoOnList(listObj)
848		}
849		metrics.RecordListCacheMetrics(c.groupResource, indexUsed, len(resp.Items), listVal.Len())
850		return nil
851	}

854	func baseObjectThreadUnsafe(object runtime.Object) runtime.Object {
855		if co, ok := object.(*cachingObject); ok {
856			return co.object
857		}

861	func (c *Cacher) triggerValuesThreadUnsafe(event *watchCacheEvent) ([]string, bool) {
862		if c.indexedTrigger == nil {
863			return nil, false
864		}
865	
866		result := make([]string, 0, 2)
867		result = append(result, c.indexedTrigger.indexerFunc(baseObjectThreadUnsafe(event.Object)))
868		if event.PrevObject == nil {
869			return result, true
870		}
871		prevTriggerValue := c.indexedTrigger.indexerFunc(baseObjectThreadUnsafe(event.PrevObject))
872		if result[0] != prevTriggerValue {
873			result = append(result, prevTriggerValue)
874		}
875		return result, true
876	}

879		if curLen := int64(len(c.incoming)); c.incomingHWM.Update(curLen) {
880			// Monitor if this gets backed up, and how much.
881			klog.V(1).Infof("cacher (%v): %v objects queued in incoming channel.", c.groupResource.String(), curLen)
882		}
883		c.incoming <- *event

886	func (c *Cacher) dispatchEvents() {
887		// Jitter to help level out any aggregate load.
888		bookmarkTimer := c.clock.NewTimer(wait.Jitter(time.Second, 0.25))
889		defer bookmarkTimer.Stop()
890	
891		// The internal informer populates the RV as soon as it conducts
892		// The first successful sync with the underlying store.
893		// The cache must wait until this first sync is completed to be deemed ready.
894		// Since we cannot send a bookmark when the lastProcessedResourceVersion is 0,
895		// we poll aggressively for the first list RV before entering the dispatch loop.
896		lastProcessedResourceVersion := uint64(0)
897		if err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(wait.ContextForChannel(c.stopCh), 10*time.Millisecond, true, func(_ context.Context) (bool, error) {
898			if rv := c.watchCache.getListResourceVersion(); rv != 0 {
899				lastProcessedResourceVersion = rv
900				return true, nil
901			}
902			return false, nil
903		}); err != nil {
904			// given the function above never returns error,
905			// the non-empty error means that the stopCh was closed
906			return
907		}
908		for {
909			select {
910			case event, ok := <-c.incoming:
911				if !ok {
912					return
913				}
914				// Don't dispatch bookmarks coming from the storage layer.
915				// They can be very frequent (even to the level of subseconds)
916				// to allow efficient watch resumption on kube-apiserver restarts,
917				// and propagating them down may overload the whole system.
918				//
919				// TODO: If at some point we decide the performance and scalability
920				// footprint is acceptable, this is the place to hook them in.
921				// However, we then need to check if this was called as a result
922				// of a bookmark event or regular Add/Update/Delete operation by
923				// checking if resourceVersion here has changed.
924				if event.Type != watch.Bookmark {
925					c.dispatchEvent(&event)
926				}
927				lastProcessedResourceVersion = event.ResourceVersion
928				metrics.EventsCounter.WithLabelValues(c.groupResource.Group, c.groupResource.Resource).Inc()
929			case <-bookmarkTimer.C():
930				bookmarkTimer.Reset(wait.Jitter(time.Second, 0.25))
931				bookmarkEvent := &watchCacheEvent{
932					Type:            watch.Bookmark,
933					Object:          c.newFunc(),
934					ResourceVersion: lastProcessedResourceVersion,
935				}
936				if err := c.versioner.UpdateObject(bookmarkEvent.Object, bookmarkEvent.ResourceVersion); err != nil {
937					klog.Errorf("failure to set resourceVersion to %d on bookmark event %+v", bookmarkEvent.ResourceVersion, bookmarkEvent.Object)
938					continue
939				}
940				c.dispatchEvent(bookmarkEvent)
941			case <-c.stopCh:
942				return
943			}
944		}
945	}

947	func setCachingObjects(event *watchCacheEvent, versioner storage.Versioner) {
948		switch event.Type {
949		case watch.Added, watch.Modified:
950			if object, err := newCachingObject(event.Object); err == nil {
951				event.Object = object
952			} else {
953				klog.Errorf("couldn't create cachingObject from: %#v", event.Object)
954			}
955			// Don't wrap PrevObject for update event (for create events it is nil).
956			// We only encode those to deliver DELETE watch events, so if
957			// event.Object is not nil it can be used only for watchers for which
958			// selector was satisfied for its previous version and is no longer
959			// satisfied for the current version.
960			// This is rare enough that it doesn't justify making deep-copy of the
961			// object (done by newCachingObject) every time.
962		case watch.Deleted:
963			// Don't wrap Object for delete events - these are not to deliver any
964			// events. Only wrap PrevObject.
965			if object, err := newCachingObject(event.PrevObject); err == nil {
966				// Update resource version of the object.
967				// event.PrevObject is used to deliver DELETE watch events and
968				// for them, we set resourceVersion to <current> instead of
969				// the resourceVersion of the last modification of the object.
970				updateResourceVersion(object, versioner, event.ResourceVersion)
971				event.PrevObject = object
972			} else {
973				klog.Errorf("couldn't create cachingObject from: %#v", event.Object)
974			}
975		}
976	}
977	
978	func (c *Cacher) dispatchEvent(event *watchCacheEvent) {
979		c.startDispatching(event)
980		defer c.finishDispatching()
981		// Watchers stopped after startDispatching will be delayed to finishDispatching,
982	
983		// Since add() can block, we explicitly add when cacher is unlocked.
984		// Dispatching event in nonblocking way first, which make faster watchers
985		// not be blocked by slower ones.
986		if event.Type == watch.Bookmark {
987			for _, watcher := range c.watchersBuffer {
988				watcher.nonblockingAdd(event)
989			}
990		} else {
991			// Set up caching of object serializations only for dispatching this event.
992			//
993			// Storing serializations in memory would result in increased memory usage,
994			// but it would help for caching encodings for watches started from old
995			// versions. However, we still don't have a convincing data that the gain
996			// from it justifies increased memory usage, so for now we drop the cached
997			// serializations after dispatching this event.
998			//
999			// Given that CachingObject is just wrapping the object and not perfoming
1000			// deep-copying (until some field is explicitly being modified), we create
1001			// it unconditionally to ensure safety and reduce deep-copying.
1002			//
1003			// Make a shallow copy to allow overwriting Object and PrevObject.
1004			wcEvent := *event
1005			setCachingObjects(&wcEvent, c.versioner)
1006			event = &wcEvent
1007	
1008			c.blockedWatchers = c.blockedWatchers[:0]
1009			for _, watcher := range c.watchersBuffer {
1010				if !watcher.nonblockingAdd(event) {
1011					c.blockedWatchers = append(c.blockedWatchers, watcher)
1012				}
1013			}
1014	
1015			if len(c.blockedWatchers) > 0 {
1016				// dispatchEvent is called very often, so arrange
1017				// to reuse timers instead of constantly allocating.
1018				startTime := time.Now()
1019				timeout := c.dispatchTimeoutBudget.takeAvailable()
1020				c.timer.Reset(timeout)
1021	
1022				// Send event to all blocked watchers. As long as timer is running,
1023				// `add` will wait for the watcher to unblock. After timeout,
1024				// `add` will not wait, but immediately close a still blocked watcher.
1025				// Hence, every watcher gets the chance to unblock itself while timer
1026				// is running, not only the first ones in the list.
1027				timer := c.timer
1028				for _, watcher := range c.blockedWatchers {
1029					if !watcher.add(event, timer) {
1030						// fired, clean the timer by set it to nil.
1031						timer = nil
1032					}
1033				}
1034	
1035				// Stop the timer if it is not fired
1036				if timer != nil && !timer.Stop() {
1037					// Consume triggered (but not yet received) timer event
1038					// so that future reuse does not get a spurious timeout.
1039					<-timer.C
1040				}
1041	
1042				c.dispatchTimeoutBudget.returnUnused(timeout - time.Since(startTime))
1043			}
1044		}
1045	}

1047	func (c *Cacher) startDispatchingBookmarkEventsLocked() {
1048		// Pop already expired watchers. However, explicitly ignore stopped ones,
1049		// as we don't delete watcher from bookmarkWatchers when it is stopped.
1050		for _, watchers := range c.bookmarkWatchers.popExpiredWatchersThreadUnsafe() {
1051			for _, watcher := range watchers {
1052				// c.Lock() is held here.
1053				// watcher.stopThreadUnsafe() is protected by c.Lock()
1054				if watcher.stopped {
1055					continue
1056				}
1057				c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1058				c.expiredBookmarkWatchers = append(c.expiredBookmarkWatchers, watcher)
1059			}
1060		}
1061	}
1062	
1063	// startDispatching chooses watchers potentially interested in a given event
1064	// a marks dispatching as true.
1065	func (c *Cacher) startDispatching(event *watchCacheEvent) {
1066		// It is safe to call triggerValuesThreadUnsafe here, because at this
1067		// point only this thread can access this event (we create a separate
1068		// watchCacheEvent for every dispatch).
1069		triggerValues, supported := c.triggerValuesThreadUnsafe(event)
1070	
1071		c.Lock()
1072		defer c.Unlock()
1073	
1074		c.dispatching = true
1075		// We are reusing the slice to avoid memory reallocations in every
1076		// dispatchEvent() call. That may prevent Go GC from freeing items
1077		// from previous phases that are sitting behind the current length
1078		// of the slice, but there is only a limited number of those and the
1079		// gain from avoiding memory allocations is much bigger.
1080		c.watchersBuffer = c.watchersBuffer[:0]
1081	
1082		if event.Type == watch.Bookmark {
1083			c.startDispatchingBookmarkEventsLocked()
1084			// return here to reduce following code indentation and diff
1085			return
1086		}
1087	
1088		// iterate over watchers for each applicable namespace/name tuple
1089		namespace := event.ObjFields["metadata.namespace"]
1090		name := event.ObjFields["metadata.name"]
1091		if len(namespace) > 0 {
1092			if len(name) > 0 {
1093				// namespaced watchers scoped by name
1094				for _, watcher := range c.watchers.allWatchers[namespacedName{namespace: namespace, name: name}] {
1095					c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1096				}
1097			}
1098			// namespaced watchers not scoped by name
1099			for _, watcher := range c.watchers.allWatchers[namespacedName{namespace: namespace}] {
1100				c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1101			}
1102		}
1103		if len(name) > 0 {
1104			// cluster-wide watchers scoped by name
1105			for _, watcher := range c.watchers.allWatchers[namespacedName{name: name}] {
1106				c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1107			}
1108		}
1109		// cluster-wide watchers unscoped by name
1110		for _, watcher := range c.watchers.allWatchers[namespacedName{}] {
1111			c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1112		}
1113	
1114		if supported {
1115			// Iterate over watchers interested in the given values of the trigger.
1116			for _, triggerValue := range triggerValues {
1117				for _, watcher := range c.watchers.valueWatchers[triggerValue] {
1118					c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1119				}
1120			}
1121		} else {
1122			// supported equal to false generally means that trigger function
1123			// is not defined (or not aware of any indexes). In this case,
1124			// watchers filters should generally also don't generate any
1125			// trigger values, but can cause problems in case of some
1126			// misconfiguration. Thus we paranoidly leave this branch.
1127	
1128			// Iterate over watchers interested in exact values for all values.
1129			for _, watchers := range c.watchers.valueWatchers {
1130				for _, watcher := range watchers {
1131					c.watchersBuffer = append(c.watchersBuffer, watcher)
1132				}
1133			}
1134		}
1135	}
1136	
1137	// finishDispatching stops all the watchers that were supposed to be
1138	// stopped in the meantime, but it was deferred to avoid closing input
1139	// channels of watchers, as add() may still have writing to it.
1140	// It also marks dispatching as false.
1141	func (c *Cacher) finishDispatching() {
1142		c.Lock()
1143		defer c.Unlock()
1144		c.dispatching = false
1145		for _, watcher := range c.watchersToStop {
1146			watcher.stopLocked()
1147		}
1148		c.watchersToStop = c.watchersToStop[:0]
1149	
1150		for _, watcher := range c.expiredBookmarkWatchers {
1151			if watcher.stopped {
1152				continue
1153			}
1154			// requeue the watcher for the next bookmark if needed.
1155			c.bookmarkWatchers.addWatcherThreadUnsafe(watcher)
1156		}
1157		c.expiredBookmarkWatchers = c.expiredBookmarkWatchers[:0]
1158	}

1166	func (c *Cacher) stopWatcherLocked(watcher *cacheWatcher) {
1167		if c.dispatching {
1168			c.watchersToStop = append(c.watchersToStop, watcher)
1169		} else {
1170			watcher.stopLocked()
1171		}
1172	}

1193	// Stop implements the graceful termination.
1194	func (c *Cacher) Stop() {
1195		c.stopLock.Lock()
1196		if c.stopped {
1197			// avoid stopping twice (note: cachers are shared with subresources)
1198			c.stopLock.Unlock()
1199			return
1200		}
1201		c.stopped = true
1202		c.ready.stop()
1203		c.stopLock.Unlock()
1204		close(c.stopCh)
1205		c.stopWg.Wait()
1206	}

1212	func forgetWatcher(c *Cacher, w *cacheWatcher, index int, scope namespacedName, triggerValue string, triggerSupported bool) func(bool) {
1213		return func(drainWatcher bool) {
1214			c.Lock()
1215			defer c.Unlock()
1216	
1217			w.setDrainInputBufferLocked(drainWatcher)
1218	
1219			// It's possible that the watcher is already not in the structure (e.g. in case of
1220			// simultaneous Stop() and terminateAllWatchers(), but it is safe to call stopLocked()
1221			// on a watcher multiple times.

1227	func filterWithAttrsAndPrefixFunction(key string, p storage.SelectionPredicate, groupResource schema.GroupResource) filterWithAttrsFunc {
1228		isSharded := utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ShardedListAndWatch) && p.ShardSelector != nil && !p.ShardSelector.Empty()
1229		filterFunc := func(objKey string, label labels.Set, field fields.Set, obj runtime.Object) bool {
1230			if !hasPathPrefix(objKey, key) {
1231				return false
1232			}
1233			if isSharded {
1234				matches, err := p.MatchesSharding(obj)
1235				if err != nil {
1236					utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("shard matching failed for %v: %w", groupResource, err))
1237					return false
1238				}
1239				if !matches {
1240					metrics.RecordWatchFilteredEvent(groupResource)
1241					return false
1242				}
1243			}
1244			return p.MatchesObjectAttributes(label, field)
1245		}
1246		return filterFunc
1247	}

1284	// getWatchCacheResourceVersion returns a ResourceVersion to which the watch cache must be synchronized to
1285	//
1286	// Depending on the input parameters, the semantics of the returned ResourceVersion are:
1287	//   - must be at Exact RV (when parsedWatchResourceVersion > 0)
1288	//   - can be at Any RV (when parsedWatchResourceVersion = 0)
1289	//   - must be at Most Recent RV (return an RV from etcd)
1290	//
1291	// note that the above semantic is enforced by the API validation (defined elsewhere):
1292	//
1293	//	if SendInitiaEvents != nil => ResourceVersionMatch = NotOlderThan
1294	//	if ResourceVersionmatch != nil => ResourceVersionMatch = NotOlderThan & SendInitialEvents != nil
1295	func (c *Cacher) getWatchCacheResourceVersion(ctx context.Context, parsedWatchResourceVersion uint64, opts storage.ListOptions) (uint64, error) {
1296		if len(opts.ResourceVersion) != 0 {
1297			return parsedWatchResourceVersion, nil
1298		}
1299		// legacy case
1300		if opts.SendInitialEvents == nil && opts.ResourceVersion == "" {
1301			return 0, nil
1302		}
1303		rv, err := c.storage.GetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx)
1304		return rv, err
1305	}
